petitioner_background: The petitioner is a self-represented individual seeking classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability. No specific field of work, nationality, or career details are provided in the decision. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 05 060 52323).
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because the petitioner failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). The appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing the pertinent regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3), and the petitioner indicated he was not submitting a separate brief or evidence. The petitioner's brief statement on appeal merely asserted a distinguished reputation and requested reconsideration without addressing the director's specific reasons for denial.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) for failure to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact on appeal. The petitioner submitted no brief, arguments, or additional evidence addressing the regulatory criteria or the director's reasons for denial.
